title: EEG signal quality: garbage in, garbage out
preview: Before any ML model touches EEG data, the preprocessing pipeline decides everything. Most attention detection failures are not model failures...

detail.body:
EEG data is extraordinarily noisy. A 60 Hz power line artifact can completely drown out the 8-12 Hz alpha band activity that indicates relaxed attention. Eye blinks create voltage spikes 10x larger than the neural signals we care about. Muscle activity near the electrodes creates broadband noise.

Our preprocessing pipeline: notch filter at 60 Hz and harmonics, bandpass filter 1-50 Hz, independent component analysis to remove eye-blink artifacts, epoch rejection for remaining artifact-contaminated segments.

After clean preprocessing, a simple SVM on band-power features achieved 78% accuracy. Before preprocessing, the same SVM on raw data achieved 51% — barely above chance. The model was not the bottleneck. The data quality was.
